item_a.name: Honda HRX217VKA Self-Propelled Lawn Mower
item_a.image_alt: Honda HRX217VKA self-propelled walk-behind lawn mower with red body and black wheels
pros: Legendary Honda GCV200 engine with 10-year warranty
Versamow System allows mulching and bagging simultaneously
MicroCut twin-blade system produces ultra-fine clippings
Cruise control for consistent mowing speed
cons: Smaller 21-inch cutting deck requires more passes
Higher price point than comparable models
Heavier weight at 89 pounds makes transport more difficult
item_a.best_for: Homeowners with medium-sized lawns who prioritize reliability, superior mulching performance, and long-term engine durability.

item_b.name: Toro TimeMaster 30-Inch Self-Propelled Lawn Mower
item_b.image_alt: Toro TimeMaster 30-inch wide cut walk-behind lawn mower with red body and extra-wide deck
pros: Extra-wide 30-inch cutting deck cuts mowing time nearly in half
Personal Pace self-propel system adjusts to your walking speed
Powerful Briggs & Stratton 223cc engine handles thick grass
Dual-force cutting system with floating deck for uneven terrain
cons: Larger footprint makes storage more challenging
Heavier at 127 pounds, difficult for some users to maneuver
More expensive maintenance due to larger deck and dual-blade system
item_b.best_for: Homeowners with large lawns (half-acre or more) who want to significantly reduce mowing time and don't mind the extra weight.

comparison_summary: The Honda HRX217VKA and Toro TimeMaster serve different yard sizes and priorities. Honda offers superior mulching technology and legendary engine reliability in a standard 21-inch format, while the TimeMaster's 30-inch deck cuts mowing time nearly in half for larger properties. Your choice depends primarily on lawn size and whether you value time savings over maneuverability.
verdict_text: The Honda HRX217VKA wins for small-to-medium lawns prioritizing quality and durability, while the Toro TimeMaster is unbeatable for large properties where time efficiency matters most.
seo_content:
When comparing the Honda HRX217VKA vs Toro TimeMaster, you're looking at two premium walk-behind mowers designed for fundamentally different user needs. The Toro TimeMaster vs Honda HRX217VKA debate ultimately comes down to lawn size, budget, and what you value most in a mower.

The Honda HRX217VKA represents the gold standard in 21-inch residential mowers. Its legendary GCV200 engine comes with an industry-leading 10-year warranty, reflecting Honda's confidence in their small engine technology. The MicroCut twin-blade system creates four cutting surfaces that produce exceptionally fine clippings, perfect for mulching. Honda's exclusive Versamow System gives you unprecedented control over clipping disposal—you can mulch, bag, discharge, or do any combination simultaneously by simply adjusting a lever. At 89 pounds with cruise control, it's manageable for most users and ideal for lawns up to half an acre.

The Toro TimeMaster takes a completely different approach with its massive 30-inch cutting deck—that's 43% wider than the Honda. For homeowners with large properties (half-acre and up), this translates to cutting mowing time nearly in half. The 223cc Briggs & Stratton engine delivers more raw power to handle the wider cutting path, while the Personal Pace self-propel system intuitively matches your walking speed. The TimeMaster's 4-bushel bag capacity means fewer stops to empty clippings compared to Honda's 2.5-bushel bag.

Price differentiation is significant: the Honda costs $749-$849 while the TimeMaster runs $1,199-$1,399. However, the TimeMaster's time savings could justify the premium if you're mowing a large lawn weekly. Consider that cutting 30-40 minutes per mowing session saves approximately 20-25 hours annually for weekly mowing over a 30-week season.

Maneuverability favors the Honda. At 127 pounds, the TimeMaster requires more physical effort to turn and push, especially on slopes. It also demands more storage space. The Honda navigates tight spaces, gates, and landscaping obstacles more easily. For lawns under half an acre with multiple beds, trees, or narrow passages, the Honda's compact footprint is advantageous.

Both offer excellent build quality and cutting performance. Your decision should prioritize lawn size first: properties over half an acre benefit enormously from the TimeMaster's efficiency, while smaller lawns are better served by the Honda's precision, reliability, and lower cost. Neither choice disappoints—they're simply optimized for different scenarios.

faq.q: Is the Toro TimeMaster worth the extra cost over a Honda mower?
faq.a: For lawns over half an acre, yes. The TimeMaster's 30-inch deck cuts mowing time by 40-50% compared to a 21-inch Honda, potentially saving 20+ hours per season. For smaller lawns under half an acre, the Honda's lower price and superior maneuverability make it the better value.

faq.q: Which mower is better for uneven or hilly terrain?
faq.a: The Honda HRX217VKA is better for hills and slopes due to its lighter weight (89 vs 127 pounds) and more compact size. The TimeMaster's extra weight makes it challenging on inclines, though its dual-force floating deck handles surface irregularities well on flat terrain.

faq.q: How does the Honda engine warranty compare to the Toro TimeMaster?
faq.a: Honda offers a 10-year warranty on the GCV200 engine, significantly longer than the 2-year warranty on the TimeMaster's Briggs & Stratton engine. However, Toro provides a 3-year full warranty on the complete mower versus Honda's 3-year residential warranty on non-engine components.

faq.q: Can the Toro TimeMaster fit through standard gates?
faq.a: The TimeMaster's 30-inch cutting width plus handles makes it approximately 32-34 inches wide total. Standard residential gates are typically 36 inches, so it will fit but with little clearance. Measure your narrowest gate before purchasing, as the Honda's 21-inch deck navigates gates much more easily.

faq.q: Which mower produces better mulching results?
faq.a: The Honda HRX217VKA produces finer mulched clippings thanks to its MicroCut twin-blade system with four cutting surfaces and slower blade tip speed. The TimeMaster mulches adequately but prioritizes speed and efficiency over ultra-fine clipping size. For premium mulching quality, Honda has the edge.

meta_title: Honda HRX217VKA vs Toro TimeMaster (2026): Which Should You Buy?
meta_description: Detailed comparison of Honda HRX217VKA vs Toro TimeMaster. We break down cutting width, engine reliability, mowing time, and value to help you pick the right lawn mower.
